skill.name: testing-strategy
skill.description: Guides teams through test-first development with real implementations, integration tests, and Playwright E2E coverage.
skill.summary: Plan test-first delivery with TDD, integration coverage, and E2E validation.
skill.author: Doyajin174

security_audit.summary: AI review dismissed all external command alerts as Markdown fence markers and inert code examples. The weak cryptographic algorithm alert at SKILL.md:3 is a false positive in descriptive text, with no cryptographic API use found. No prompt injection, network access, filesystem abuse, or data exfiltration evidence was found.

content.value_statement: Teams often add tests after code, which can hide design gaps. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through TDD, integration tests, and E2E checks.
actual_capabilities: Explains a red, green, refactor workflow for feature development.
Encourages writing a failing test before production code.
Promotes real implementations over broad test mocks.
Shows when to use unit, integration, and E2E test categories.
Includes Playwright guidance for key user journeys.
Provides a checklist for test coverage review.
limitations: Does not provide runnable test automation scripts.
Does not configure specific test runners or CI systems.
Uses brief examples instead of full project templates.
May need adaptation for teams that require heavy mocking.

use_cases.title: Plan a New Feature
use_cases.description: Define the first failing test and expected behavior before implementation starts.

use_cases.title: Improve Integration Coverage
use_cases.description: Replace broad mocks with real services, test databases, or HTTP layer simulation.

use_cases.title: Validate User Journeys
use_cases.description: Choose critical flows for Playwright E2E coverage and align them with release risk.

prompt_templates.title: Start With a Failing Test
prompt_templates.prompt: Use testing-strategy to plan the first failing test for this feature: [feature]. Include expected behavior and the first assertion.

prompt_templates.title: Build Integration Coverage
prompt_templates.prompt: Use testing-strategy to design integration tests for [service]. Prefer real implementations and explain any allowed HTTP simulation.

prompt_templates.title: Add E2E Protection
prompt_templates.prompt: Use testing-strategy to identify three Playwright E2E flows for [product area]. Rank them by user impact and regression risk.

prompt_templates.title: Review a Test Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Use testing-strategy to audit this test plan: [plan]. Find missing red tests, overused mocks, and uncovered E2E paths.

output_examples.input: Create a test strategy for a checkout feature.
output: Start with a failing test for successful checkout confirmation.
Add integration tests for cart, payment, and order persistence.
Protect the main purchase path with a Playwright E2E scenario.

output_examples.input: Review tests for a user profile service.
output: Flag broad database mocks that hide persistence behavior.
Recommend a test database or in-memory implementation.
Keep focused unit tests for pure validation logic.

output_examples.input: Choose E2E tests for a release.
output: Prioritize login, checkout, and account recovery flows.
Tie each flow to a user-visible failure mode.
Avoid duplicating every unit test in the browser layer.

best_practices: Confirm each new production behavior with a failing test first.
Use real implementations when they give reliable, meaningful feedback.
Keep E2E coverage focused on the highest value user paths.
anti_patterns: Writing production code before a failing test defines the behavior.
Mocking core collaborators so heavily that integration behavior is untested.
Using E2E tests for every small branch instead of critical workflows.

faq.question: Does this skill generate test code automatically?
faq.answer: It guides test strategy and examples, but it does not ship runnable automation scripts.

faq.question: Can teams still use mocks?
faq.answer: The skill discourages broad mocks and permits HTTP simulation when it preserves meaningful integration behavior.

faq.question: Which tools does it mention?
faq.answer: It references Vitest, Jest, real databases, MSW, and Playwright as testing options.

faq.question: Is this only for frontend projects?
faq.answer: No. It covers unit, integration, and E2E thinking for frontend and backend systems.

faq.question: When should Playwright be used?
faq.answer: Use Playwright for critical user journeys where browser-level validation reduces release risk.

faq.question: Does it replace a QA process?
faq.answer: No. It helps structure developer testing and should support broader quality and release practices.
